# Meet Aaron: the Aficionado of Account Management

_This post is part of a bi-weekly series in which we get to know Sifties._

Aaron Ashworth is an account manager…

![Sift Author Logo](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%20504%20504'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

Sift

Nov 17, 2017

![black-dot](https://sift.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/black-dot.svg)

![Press-Release-Tile-Image-Color-Pills\_Blue](data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg%20xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'%20viewBox='0%200%201024%20683'%3E%3C/svg%3E)

_This post is part of a bi-weekly series in which we get to know Sifties._

* * *

Aaron Ashworth is an account manager who has been with Sift Science for just shy of a year. If he could eat just one food for the rest of his life, it would be sandwiches — specifically club sandwiches.

### What skill would you love to learn?

Woodworking. I’d love to make dressers or beds for my whole family, and I think it’d be especially fun to construct a built-in entertainment unit.

### What superpower would you want to have and why?

Teleportation — because when you’ve had a long night and you have to fly somewhere, it’s not the best.

### I hear that you’re really into cars. What’s your dream car?

That’s really tough right now. It always changes, depending on new models that come out. My top three would be a McLaren P1, Ferrari LaFerrari, and Lamborghini Aventador. If I had to choose an attainable sports car, it would be a Corvette.

### What was your first car?

A 1996 Dodge Stratus with subwoofers. I got interested in cars when I was five years old and my friend’s dad bought a 1989 Corvette. I was awestruck.

### What was your first job?

Blockbuster video. I was a cashier in high school for two years, making $5.15 an hour. My favorite thing to do was shrink-wrap the movies.
